name: Queue saturation

injection.description: Raise offered load above the measured saturation knee (e.g. bursty workload at a multiple of knee arrival rate) so the admission queue reaches its configured bound.

expected_gateway_semantics: Excess requests are shed at admission with 429 + Retry-After and a typed error envelope (error.type=rate_limited) — shedding is explicit, never silent queue growth or connection resets.
Accepted-request latency is protected: queue depth and queue wait stay at their configured bounds; requests already admitted keep meeting the latency SLO.
expected_client_visible_behavior: Shed requests receive HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header and a typed rate_limited envelope carrying the request ID.
Accepted requests are indistinguishable from no-fault operation (latency within SLO).

metrics_that_must_move.expectation: increases with reason=queue_full while the overload lasts
plateaus at the configured bound instead of growing unbounded
p95 for admitted requests stays within the configured admission bound

metrics_that_must_not_move.expectation: p95 of admitted requests stays within the SLO — shedding protects the admitted population

abort_condition: Abort the injection if accepted-request TTFT p95 exceeds the SLO threshold by more than 50% (shedding failed to protect admitted traffic), or if queue depth grows past its configured bound.
